parameters.text:
=You are a recipe curator for LowKeyCooking — a food blog for home cooks.
Brand values: budget-conscious, comfort-first, accessible, globally-inspired classics.
"Great food is a right for every home cook."

CURRENT COLLECTION:
{{ $json.history_summary }}

SEASON: {{ $json.season }}

EXCLUDED SLUGS (must not generate any of these):
{{ $json.exclude_slugs }}

CONSTRAINTS (apply to every candidate):
- Max 30 minutes total (prep + cook combined)
- Beginner skill — achievable by someone who rarely cooks
- Grocery-store ingredients only (Walmart, Kroger, Target level)
- No luxury ingredients: no truffle, lobster, wagyu, burrata, or expensive specialty items
- No tofu
- Fish must be tilapia, salmon, or mahi mahi only
- Target 6-8 ingredients, hard max 10
- Single cooking vessel only (one pan, one pot, or one baking sheet)
- Max 6 steps — no special techniques (no deep frying, no emulsifying, no dough-making)

Your task:
1. Analyze the collection above. What cuisines, meal types, and proteins are missing or underrepresented?
2. Generate exactly 3 diverse recipe candidates that fill the identified gaps and fit the current season.
3. Select the single best candidate: most novel vs the existing collection, best seasonal fit, strongest brand alignment.

Avoid generic names — be specific (e.g. not "Chicken Stir Fry" but "Ginger Scallion Chicken Stir Fry").

Return ONLY valid JSON, no prose, no code fences:
{
  "gap_analysis": "one sentence describing what is missing from the collection",
  "candidates": [
    { "recipe_name": "string", "slug": "kebab-case", "cuisine": "string", "meal_type": "string", "protein": "string", "why": "string" },
    { "recipe_name": "string", "slug": "kebab-case", "cuisine": "string", "meal_type": "string", "protein": "string", "why": "string" },
    { "recipe_name": "string", "slug": "kebab-case", "cuisine": "string", "meal_type": "string", "protein": "string", "why": "string" }
  ],
  "selected": {
    "recipe_name": "string",
    "slug": "kebab-case",
    "cuisine": "string",
    "meal_type": "string",
    "protein": "string",
    "why_fit": "one sentence on why this is the best addition to the collection right now"
  }
}

parameters.jsCode:
const recipes = [
  'Lemon Butter Salmon',
  'Shakshuka',
  'Beef Bulgogi Rice Bowl',
  'Roasted Tomato Soup',
  'Crispy Chickpea Stir Fry',
  'Lemon Ricotta Pancakes',
  'Chicken Tikka Masala',
  'Caprese Pasta',
  'Korean Fried Chicken',
  'Smashed Crispy Burger',
  'Honey Garlic Glazed Shrimp',
  'Lamb Kofta with Tzatziki',
  'Harissa Roasted Cauliflower',
  'Birria Quesatacos',
  'Classic Banana Bread',
  'One-Pan Garlic Butter Shrimp Pasta',
  'Creamy Tuscan Chicken',
  'Sheet Pan Fajitas',
  'Egg Fried Rice',
  'Black Bean Tacos',
  'Greek Lemon Chicken',
  'Teriyaki Salmon Bowl',
  'Creamy Tomato Tortellini',
  'Cheesy Baked Chicken Thighs',
  'Sesame Noodles',
  'Skillet Cornbread',
  'Classic Brownies',
  'Mango Chicken Curry',
  'Pork Fried Rice',
  'Vegetable Minestrone'
];

const recipe = recipes[Math.floor(Math.random() * recipes.length)];
const slug = recipe.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '');

return [{ json: { recipe_name: recipe, slug, why_fit: 'fallback_random_recipe', cuisine: '', meal_type: '', protein: '' } }];

parameters.text: ={{ '🎉 Published!\n\n' + $('Build Preview URL1').first().json.recipeName + ' is now live on the site.' }}
={{ '❌ Recipe Rejected\n\n' + $('Build Preview URL1').first().json.recipeName + ' has been discarded and will not be published.' }}

=You are a professional food blogger for LowKeyCooking. Write a complete recipe markdown file in English for "{{ $json.recipe_name }}".

Use this image URL exactly as-is in the frontmatter image field: {{ $json.image_url }}

Your response must begin with this HTML comment on the very first line (replace YOUR-SLUG with the kebab-case slug of the recipe name):
<!-- filename: YOUR-SLUG.md -->

Then immediately output the markdown with NO code fences, NO preamble, and NO explanation. Follow this exact structure:

---
title: [Recipe Name]
description: [One engaging, appetizing sentence about the dish]
image: {{ $json.image_url }}
category: [one of: breakfast, lunch, dinner, dessert]
prep_time: [e.g. "15 mins"]
cook_time: [e.g. "20 mins"]
servings: [number]
utensils:
  - name: [Utensil Name]
    url: https://www.amazon.com/s?k=[utensil+name+url+encoded]&tag=lowkeycooking-20
nutrition:
  calories: [integer]
  protein: [e.g. "25g"]
  carbs: [e.g. "30g"]
  fat: [e.g. "10g"]
  fiber: [e.g. "3g"]
  sugar: [e.g. "5g"]
  sodium: [e.g. "400mg"]
---

[1-2 sentence warm personal intro paragraph about why you love this recipe]

## Ingredients

- [ingredient with quantity]
[6-10 total ingredients, max 12]

## Instructions

1. [First step]
2. [Second step]
[Continue numbering...]

Guidelines:
- Use only common grocery-store ingredients (Walmart, Kroger, Target level)
- Total prep + cook time must be under 30 minutes
- Maximum 6 numbered instruction steps — combine steps if needed
- Use only one pan, pot, or baking sheet — no juggling multiple vessels
- No special techniques: no tempering, no deep frying, no emulsifying, no dough
- Write in a warm, approachable tone for someone who rarely cooks
- Keep sentences short and direct in the instructions
- Do NOT wrap the output in markdown code fences
- Do NOT add any text before the filename comment or after the last instruction

=Eres un blogger de cocina profesional para LowKeyCooking. Escribe un archivo markdown completo de receta en ESPAÑOL para "{{ $json.recipe_name }}".

Usa esta URL de imagen exactamente tal como está en el campo image del frontmatter: {{ $json.image_url }}

Tu respuesta debe comenzar con este comentario HTML en la primera línea (reemplaza TU-SLUG con el slug en kebab-case del nombre de la receta en español):
<!-- filename: TU-SLUG.md -->

Luego escribe el markdown inmediatamente SIN bloques de código, SIN preámbulo y SIN explicaciones. Sigue esta estructura exacta:

---
title: [Nombre de la receta en español]
description: [Una oración atractiva y apetitosa sobre el plato]
image: {{ $json.image_url }}
category: [uno de: breakfast, lunch, dinner, dessert]
prep_time: [ej. "15 min"]
cook_time: [ej. "20 min"]
servings: [número]
utensils:
  - name: [Nombre del utensilio en español]
    url: https://www.amazon.com/s?k=[utensil+name+url+encoded]&tag=lowkeycooking-20
nutrition:
  calories: [entero]
  protein: [ej. "25g"]
  carbs: [ej. "30g"]
  fat: [ej. "10g"]
  fiber: [ej. "3g"]
  sugar: [ej. "5g"]
  sodium: [ej. "400mg"]
---

[1-2 oraciones cálidas de introducción personal sobre por qué te encanta esta receta]

## Ingredientes

- [ingrediente con cantidad]
[6-10 ingredientes en total, máximo 12]

## Instrucciones

1. [Primer paso]
2. [Segundo paso]
[Continuar numerando...]

Pautas:
- Usa solo ingredientes comunes de supermercado (nivel Walmart, Kroger, Target)
- El tiempo total de preparación y cocción debe ser menor de 30 minutos
- Máximo 6 pasos numerados — combina pasos si es necesario
- Usa solo una sartén, olla o bandeja de horno — sin malabarismos con varios utensilios
- Sin técnicas especiales: sin frituras profundas, sin masas, sin emulsionar
- Escribe con un tono cálido y accesible para alguien que cocina poco
- Mantén las oraciones cortas y directas en las instrucciones
- TODO el contenido (título, descripción, utensilios, pasos) debe estar en ESPAÑOL
- NO envuelvas la salida en bloques de código markdown
- NO añadas ningún texto antes del comentario filename ni después del último paso
